summary.intent: Clean up the test suite by removing a low-value test file (checkpoint_action_test.zsh) that had excessive setup complexity for trivial assertions.
summary.outcome: checkpoint_action_test.zsh was deleted, test/run.zsh was updated to remove the reference, and the change was committed and pushed to remote (after rebasing on upstream changes).

repo: entire-fzf has a test suite under test/ with individual .zsh test files and a run.zsh runner
Tests use fake binaries and subshell stubs to isolate behavior — checkpoint_action_test.zsh required two fake binaries plus subshell setup
no_color_test.zsh and checkpoint_action_test.zsh had overlapping coverage of the fzf_input/awk formatting path; the latter tested the color variant but was largely redundant
_entire_session_fzf and _entire_action_pick were the two stubs used in the deleted test

code.finding: Test was deleted — it only verified that cut -f1 extracts field 1 and that 'entire checkpoint explain' gets called, both trivially obvious from the source
Runner file required manual update to remove the deleted test's entry

workflow: When a test's setup complexity (fake binaries, subshell stubs) outweighs the value of what it asserts, deletion is preferable to maintenance
Remote had new commits before push; rebase-then-push was used rather than merge

friction: Remote had diverged from local before push, requiring a pull/rebase step
open_items: Consider auditing other tests in the suite for similar complexity-to-value imbalance, particularly any that heavily stub _entire_session_fzf or _entire_action_pick
